commit.message:
Add README with architecture overview, setup, and API docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

commit.message:
fix: cluster pocket detection + paramilitary cleanup + combat gates

- Pocket detection now finds clusters of 1-3 connected enemy OSIDs
  (previously only detected single isolated OSIDs). Fixes Banja Luka
  HRHB cluster (dragocaj + potkozarje_3) persisting despite full RS
  encirclement.

- Paramilitary sweep: civilian casualties now properly recorded;
  instant capture (march_turns=0) for already-surrounded pockets;
  bot corps AI defers to paramilitaries for rear pocket targets.

- Rear pocket consolidation: cluster-aware auto-flip for post-week-20
  cleanup of surrounded undefended territory.

- MIN_ATTACK_PERSONNEL gate (200): depleted brigades can only defend,
  preventing death-spiral attacks.

- Militia-only attacker casualty reduction (0.15x): sweeping undefended
  settlements costs ~6 casualties instead of ~40.

- Fatigue scaling (0.5 for undefended captures vs 2.0 for real combat).

- OOB spawn fixes: 4th Ozren and 505th Vitezka moved to non-encircled
  home OSIDs.

n326: ATH 85.8%, RS=26 para deployments, RBiH=7, 33 total captures.
Paramilitary civilian casualties: +2,300 (historically accurate).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(STATUS.md): Add comprehensive Classes API fix documentation

## Summary
Documents the complete resolution of the Classes API schema mismatch issue
that was preventing all class CRUD operations from working.

## Root Cause
Drizzle schema (academic.ts) defined `primaryCoursebookId` column from
planned migration FRESH_0028, but the column didn't exist in the database.
Queries using `.select()` or `.returning()` without explicit columns failed
with PostgreSQL "column does not exist" errors.

## Fixes Applied
1. route.ts (POST): `.returning()` now specifies 22 explicit columns
2. [id]/route.ts (PUT): `.select()` specifies 12 columns, `.returning()` 20 columns
3. [id]/route.ts (PATCH): `.select()` specifies 2 columns, `.returning()` 20 columns
4. [id]/route.ts (DELETE): `.select()` specifies 1 column (id only)
5. Database: Added missing `coursebooks` table and `primary_coursebook_id` column

## Verification Results
All 6 endpoints tested and working:
- GET /api/admin/classes (list with pagination)
- GET /api/admin/classes/[id] (single class)
- POST /api/admin/classes (create with auto-generated code)
- PUT /api/admin/classes/[id] (full update with audit logging)
- PATCH /api/admin/classes/[id] (partial update)
- DELETE /api/admin/classes/[id] (soft delete to 'cancelled')

## Related Commits
- 4e791bb: Initial fix for .select() calls
- 1adc9d0: Extended fix to .returning() calls

## Pattern Established
When Drizzle schema columns don't exist in DB, always use explicit
column selection to avoid runtime errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Improve aws_connect.sh robustness and add comprehensive documentation (#1)

* Fix bugs, improve robustness, and add documentation

- setup_aws_ec2_connect.sh: remove dead/broken code block (typo 'aw',
  invalid redirection '2<&1', break outside loop); remove '*' as a
  literal select menu item; add PEM file existence validation; add SSH
  username prompt with default 'ubuntu'; write SSH_USER to config.sh;
  use exit 1 instead of bare exit
- aws_connect.sh: guard 'source config.sh' with file-existence check;
  add --region flag to all three AWS CLI calls; quote all variables;
  replace infinite polling loop with timeout + state-machine that
  handles stopped/terminated/shutting-down states; restore SSH known_
  hosts handling via StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new and ConnectTimeout;
  use $SSH_USER from config instead of hardcoded 'ubuntu'
- Add README.md with prerequisites, setup, usage, SSH username reference,
  and compatibility notes

https://claude.ai/code/session_0158yWnsZbbUvVb6XzF1SeBL

* Fix SCRIPT_DIR paths, PEM check, race condition, and IP validation

- setup_aws_ec2_connect.sh: add SCRIPT_DIR so config.sh and chmod
  target the script's own directory regardless of CWD; resolve PEM
  file to an absolute path ($SCRIPT_DIR/$PEM_FILE) before writing it
  to config.sh, and check existence relative to SCRIPT_DIR.
- aws_connect.sh: tolerate the 'stopped' state for up to 5 seconds
  after start-instances (which is async) to avoid a false-positive
  abort; add a 2 s initial sleep for the same reason; validate
  IP_ADDRESS is non-empty and not "None" before attempting SSH.

* Fix off-by-one in stopped grace period check (-gt -> -ge)

* Fix shell injection: use printf %q to safely write user input to config.sh

* Fix code review issues: exit check, error message, read -r, EOF guard

- aws_connect.sh: check exit code of start-instances and fail fast with
  a clear error message instead of silently entering the polling loop
- aws_connect.sh: fix STOPPED_GRACE error message — said "5s" but the
  value counts polling iterations, not wall-clock seconds
- setup_aws_ec2_connect.sh: add -r flag to all three read calls to
  prevent backslash interpretation (ShellCheck best practice)
- setup_aws_ec2_connect.sh: guard against EOF/Ctrl-D on the select
  prompt leaving AWS_REGION unset, which would write an empty region
  to config.sh and cause downstream AWS CLI failures

https://claude.ai/code/session_0158yWnsZbbUvVb6XzF1SeBL

* Suppress start-instances JSON output; tolerate transient API errors in polling

- Redirect start-instances stdout to /dev/null for a cleaner UX; stderr
  is preserved so real failures still surface
- Add API_ERROR_GRACE (3 attempts) tolerance in the polling loop: transient
  network errors or throttling responses no longer abort immediately.
  Consecutive failures are counted; only 3 in a row trigger an abort.
  The counter resets on any successful API call. Unknown/empty state is
  shown as '?' in the progress dots to distinguish from normal polling.

https://claude.ai/code/session_0158yWnsZbbUvVb6XzF1SeBL

* Fix timeout bypass during API error iterations in polling loop

The continue statement in the API error branch skipped the timeout check,
allowing the loop to run past the configured 120s limit when errors were
interspersed with successful calls that reset API_ERROR_COUNT. Move the
timeout check into the error branch before continue so every iteration
is bounded by TIMEOUT regardless of whether the API call succeeded.

https://claude.ai/code/session_0158yWnsZbbUvVb6XzF1SeBL

* Validate PEM file exists at connect time, not just at setup time

If the PEM file is moved or deleted after setup, the script previously
failed with a cryptic SSH error. Now it fails early with a clear message
pointing the user back to setup_aws_ec2_connect.sh.

https://claude.ai/code/session_0158yWnsZbbUvVb6XzF1SeBL

* Retry public IP retrieval after instance reaches running state

Adds up to 3 attempts (with 2s sleep between) when fetching the public
IP address. Guards against transient API propagation delays where
describe-instances reports 'running' but PublicIpAddress hasn't
populated yet.

https://claude.ai/code/session_0158yWnsZbbUvVb6XzF1SeBL

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: aws-ec2-connect
repository.description: a shell script to connect to ec2, start instance and ssh into it through AWS cli.

commit.message:
Add Research tab with data engine, Monte Carlo, Black-Scholes, and historical backtesting

engine.js (500+ lines):
- Black-Scholes options pricing with all Greeks (delta, gamma, theta, vega, rho)
- Implied volatility solver (Newton-Raphson)
- Monte Carlo engine with GBM + Merton jump diffusion (1000+ paths)
- Portfolio Monte Carlo with Cholesky-correlated assets
- VaR, CVaR, Sharpe ratio, probability distributions
- Correlation analysis (Pearson, rolling, matrix)
- Historical events database:
  - 7 oil events: Kuwait 1990, Abqaiq 2019, COVID 2020, Russia 2022,
    Libya 2011, 2008 spike/crash, 2014 oil crash
  - 6 crypto events: 2017 bull, 2021 bull, FTX collapse, ETF approval,
    Terra/LUNA, COVID crash
  - Daily price series for each event
- Volatility database: 13 assets with 30d/90d/1y/long-term vol
- Correlation reference matrix: WTI, BTC, SPY, DXY, GLD, XLE, POL
- Macro data: Fed funds rate, DXY history
- Live API integration: CoinGecko prices, Fear & Greed index
- Backtesting engine to validate models against historical events

Research view in index.html:
- Live crypto prices panel with 24h change and market cap
- Monte Carlo simulator with fan chart (5th-95th percentile bands)
- Correlation heatmap with color-coded matrix
- Historical event explorer with interactive charts
- Black-Scholes options lab with all 12 outputs

Replaces Calc tab with Research tab in navigation.

New tools:
- tool-photograph: rpicam-still capture to photos/

fix(calls): defensive video crash — null StateFlow before dispose, try-catch in setupLocalVideo and SurfaceVideoView

  BEFORE native dispose() to prevent Compose rendering disposed tracks
- setupLocalVideo: wrapped in runCatching{} — on failure degrades to audio-only
  (sets _isVideoEnabled=false, cleans up partial state)
- SurfaceVideoView: wrapped renderer.init+addSink in try-catch in factory;
  wrapped removeSink+release in separate try-catch in onRelease

commit.message:
fix(tts): port window.playAudio to modular build (#48)

* feat(settings,tts): port missing settings sections + MiniMax TTS voice modes + voice clone

Closes #45

## Settings port
- Add ⚡ GitOps as 5th tab in Settings modal with full auto-commit config:
  commit trigger (5 options), message style (3 options), push behavior,
  N-files accumulation input, and manual Commit & Push Now button
- Port all missing AI tab sections from index-ori.html:
  - Processing Limits numeric grid (6 fields: sessionNotesLines, timelineLines,
    rateLimitTokens, rateLimitRefillRate, messageQueueSize, maxParallelAgents)
  - AI Self-Correction, Task Enforcement, Strict Completion Mode toggles
  - Queue Drain Mode radio group (consolidated / sequential)
  - Extended Thinking Mode toggle + token budget input (512–65536)
  - Plan Mode Length radio group (short / regular / long / unlimited)
  - Reference Documentation monospace textarea
- Update TTS_VOICES to full 19-voice official MiniMax English system list
- Wire all new data-field elements in _applyConfig() and _saveConfig()
- Add _toggleField() helper for server-config-bound toggles

## TTS Voice Modes
- Add ttsEnabled, ttsMode, ttsVoice, ttsSpeed, thinkingBudget to
  PERSISTENT_KEYS and config defaults (config-module.js)
- Emit ai_response_complete internal event from finishMainProcessing()
  in orchestration-module.js for TTS hook consumption
- TTS module hooks into ai_response_complete; reads ttsMode from config:
  - read-aloud: speaks AI response verbatim
  - quick-updates: ai.quickComplete() summarizes to 2–3 sentences
  - thinking-aloud: ai.quickComplete() generates reflective commentary
  - off: skip (no-op)
- Add quickComplete() to AIClient for lightweight non-streaming completions
  used by TTS summarization and reflection modes
- Fully wire ttsEnabled, ttsMode, ttsVoice, ttsSpeed in hub.js
  update_config handler, config_updated broadcast, and config_data response

## Voice Clone
- Add voice_clone_upload handler: receives base64 audio from browser via
  socket, builds multipart/form-data, POSTs to MiniMax /v1/files/upload
- Add voice_clone_create handler: POSTs to /v1/voice_clone with fileId + voiceId
- Wire socket.on('voice_clone_create/upload') in hub.js
- Add Voice Clone section to Settings AI tab: file picker, Upload Recording
  button, Voice ID input, Clone Voice button, status feedback line
- Add CSS for settings-num-grid, radio-group, settings-textarea-mono,
  voice-clone-status layout classes

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(tools,tasks,agents): tool alias registry, tier registry, hierarchical tasks

- tools-v5.js: add TOOL_ALIASES map to resolve common misnomers and
  alternative tool names to their canonical equivalents transparently
- agent-system-module.js: add TOOL_TIER_REGISTRY with explicit tier,
  category, risk-level, and description for every known tool; checked
  before the runtime switch/case in classifyApprovalTier()
- tasks-engine.js: add hierarchical task tree support up to 10 levels
  deep; new socket events: add_child_task, reparent_task, get_task_tree,
  get_task_children, get_task_breadcrumb

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: add reports/ to .gitignore

Plan-mode output files are ephemeral planning artifacts and should not
be tracked in version control.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(dev): add watch.sh — port-3031 watchdog for iTerm2

Starts the Overlord server and automatically restarts it on crash
or clean exit. Designed to run in a dedicated iTerm2 tab alongside
the main session.

Features:
- Coloured timestamped status lines (start, restart, crash, exit)
- Rapid-restart storm protection (stops after 20 crashes in <10 s)
- Streams server output to both the terminal tab and
  .overlord/watchdog.log for post-mortem inspection
- Ctrl-C sends SIGTERM to the child, waits 5 s, then SIGKILL

Usage: ./watch.sh  (from repo root)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(tts): port window.playAudio to modular build

The audio play button in chat was silently swallowed because
window.playAudio was never ported from index-ori.html to the
modular build. The typeof guard in chat.js prevented any error,
making the failure invisible.

Closes #47

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(tts): save audio relative to server root, not working dir

saveAudio() was using conv.getWorkingDirectory() as the base path,
which meant TTS files landed in the user's project directory rather
than the Overlord app's own .overlord/audio/. Express serves /audio
from __dirname/../.overlord/audio, causing 404s whenever the working
directory differed from the server root.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(tts): add clarifying comments to audio path and playback fixes

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Phase 1: package foundation for rosac v1.0 restructure

Scaffolds the new rosac Python package alongside the legacy rOSac.py
(kept for reference). Establishes the transport layer, Pydantic v2
models, config system with named profiles, and Typer CLI skeleton.

- pyproject.toml: hatchling build, pip-installable, [api] and [dev] extras
- transport/: AbstractTransport, SSHTransport (Fabric 3.x, auth bug fixed,
  auto paging suppression), APITransport (librouteros, lazy import)
- models.py: ArtifactDocument envelope, Finding, RosVersion, CollectionManifest
- config.py: pydantic-settings with ir-quick and full-audit built-in profiles
- cli.py: collect (dry-run) and analyze stubs via Typer
- 7 unit tests passing

feat: complete Epics 1-3 — environment, auth, app shell, AI infrastructure

Epic 1 (Environment & Schema):
- Supabase project config, 4 SQL migrations (core profile, application pipeline, subscriptions/notifications, pg_cron schedules)
- Next.js 16 + Tailwind CSS 4 + shadcn/ui v4 setup with Inter font
- Supabase client helpers (server, client, admin), auth middleware
- Environment variable template (.env.example)

Epic 2 (Auth & App Shell):
- Email/password auth flows: signin, signup, forgot password, reset password
- Auth layout with redirect for authenticated users
- App layout with sidebar navigation (responsive: desktop/tablet/mobile)
- All placeholder pages: dashboard, jobs, queue, tracker, settings
- Sonner toast notifications configured per design system

Epic 3 (AI Infrastructure):
- Anthropic SDK singleton, Langfuse tracing with trace.generation()
- Sentry error tracking with graceful degradation
- Agent call pattern with prompt caching and error classification
- Pipeline worker (process-pipeline) with claim/execute/enqueue loop
- Zod validators for all AI agent outputs (both Next.js and Deno versions)
- Test agent edge function for verification

commit.message:
Add WebRTC ICE health check to diagnose offer creation hang

Both tabs see each other's PEER ANNOUNCE messages but no offers are
sent. This adds an independent RTCPeerConnection test to verify ICE
candidate gathering works, which is required for Trystero's trickle:false
offer pool.

commit.message:
Fix ch-06 errors found by scan comparison

- Def 6.1 item vi: period moved inside \end{dfn} (was displaced as stray line after)
- Thm 6.2 proof: two spurious leading \cdot tokens removed from substitution expansion
- Sec 6.2: "n equation" -> "n equations" (missing plural s)
- Fig 6.8 caption: "NDFA" -> "DFA" (machine in Ex 6.12 is a DFA)
- Thm 6.4: B_{kN} -> B_{kn} (capital N should be lowercase n)
- Thm 6.4: Y_{m-l}, Y_{m+l} -> Y_{m-1}, Y_{m+1} (letter ell vs digit one, twice)
- Cor 6.3: "RSIG closed under" -> "RSIG is closed under" (missing verb "is")
- Ex 6.18: equation labels X_1/X_2 -> X_0/X_1 (off-by-one vs scan)
- Ex 6.25: b*X_2 -> b*X_1 in second equation (transcription error)

commit.message:
fix(rpc): add chain-linking verification to backfillFromPeer

Validates parent_hash continuity for every backfilled block and verifies
the last backfilled block connects to the lowest existing (anchor) block.
Prevents a malicious peer from injecting fabricated historical blocks.
